Pogostemon helferi

Pogostemon helferi ('Downoi')
DifficultyMedium
Growth RateMedium
Light NeedsMed - High
CO2Required
pH6.0 - 7.2
PositionForeground

✨ Why Ease-Aquatics Downoi?

Our Pogostemon helferi is prized for its unique, curly-edged "star" appearance. Unlike delicate tissue cultures, our stock is Pretoria-hardened, making it much more resilient to the initial melt often associated with this species when introduced to new water parameters.

Advanced Technical Info

  • Calcium Demand: ⚠️ Critical: Downoi is a rare plant that appreciates a bit of water hardness. In extremely soft water (low GH), the center of the crown can turn yellow or rot.
  • Light Response: Under intense lighting, the plant remains compact and hugs the substrate. In lower light, it will begin to stretch vertically.
  • Melting Risk: It is sensitive to sudden changes in CO2 or nitrate levels. Once planted, try to maintain stable parameters to ensure the crown remains healthy.
  • Shrimp Haven: The crinkled leaves provide massive surface area for biofilm, making it a favorite for shrimp grazing.

Care & Maintenance

  • Substrate: Heavy root feeder. A nutrient-rich aquasoil is the best way to ensure the vibrant lime-green color.
  • Trimming: Remove old outer leaves that may catch detritus. You can prune the tops if they grow too tall to encourage side-shoots.
  • Flow: Appreciates good water circulation around the leaves to prevent the buildup of organic waste in its crinkled foliage.

How to Propagate

  • Cutting: Use a sharp blade to cut the top of a healthy plant or snip off side-shoots that emerge from the main stem.
  • Replanting: Strip the bottom-most leaves and plant the cutting directly into the substrate.
  • Runners: While it doesn't send out long runners like hairgrass, it will form dense clumps that can be divided once established.

✅ Is this for you?

  • You want a unique "focal point" foreground plant.
  • You have a high-tech setup with CO2.
  • You want to recreate a natural "star-shaped" riverbed look.
